title: Emit model metadata change events from control plane
preview: Context: The model metadata schema v1 work (registry + validation) needs a single, reliable stream of change events to drive the changelog generator and Console "change history" views. Today we have ad-hoc logging and some release-engineering notes, but not an auditable, machine-consumable event source. This PR intr...
text: Context: The model metadata schema v1 work (registry + validation) needs a single, reliable stream of change events to drive the changelog generator and Console "change history" views. Today we have ad-hoc logging and some release-engineering notes, but not an auditable, machine-consumable event source. This PR introduces a canonical event envelope and emits two primary event types from the control plane whenever (a) model metadata is updated, and (b) a model version is promoted within an environment/deployment scope (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). It also implements idempotency + dedupe so consumers can safely replay. Summary of changes: 1) New event types and schema (v1): - redwood.model_registry.model_metadata_changed.v1 - redwood.model_registry.model_version_promoted.v1 Event envelope fields are consistent with our other platform events: event_id (UUID), occurred_at, emitted_at, actor...

title: Changelog generator: initial implementation + templates (public/internal filtering)
preview: Summary: Introduces the initial version of the changelog generator used to produce customer-visible model release notes (Console + docs) and internal audit-focused notes from model metadata change events. This adds (1) an event ingestion layer (with idempotency), (2) category mapping + severity normalization, (3) a ...
text: Summary: Introduces the initial version of the changelog generator used to produce customer-visible model release notes (Console + docs) and internal audit-focused notes from model metadata change events. This adds (1) an event ingestion layer (with idempotency), (2) category mapping + severity normalization, (3) a templating system aligned to the customer release notes taxonomy/style guide, and (4) strict public vs internal field filtering/redaction. Motivation: Today model/version changes are visible inconsistently across model registry, runtime config, and ad-hoc docs. Support escalations (e.g., SUP-28411) show we need a single, consistent change artifact generated from the same events that drive rollouts. This PR is the first end-to-end piece: given a stream of ModelMetadataChanged/ModelVersionPromoted events, produce deterministic changelog entries and expose them via an internal...
links: ADR-0143 Changelog generation from events vs git tags
ENG-9823 Changelog generator service internal API
ENG-9825 Public vs internal fields redaction rules
SUP-28411 Release notes missing for model version promotion

title: Tag eval runs with model version + model-metadata schema hash
preview: Context: As part of the Model Metadata Schema v1 + automated changelog work, we need to be able to trace a customer-visible release note entry back to concrete eval evidence (and vice versa). Today the eval harness records the model identifier and some run params, but does not persist (a) the promoted model version ...
text: Context: As part of the Model Metadata Schema v1 + automated changelog work, we need to be able to trace a customer-visible release note entry back to concrete eval evidence (and vice versa). Today the eval harness records the model identifier and some run params, but does not persist (a) the promoted model version string used for serving (e.g., catalog version/variant) and (b) the model-metadata schema hash that the changelog generator references. That makes it hard to answer support questions like “which exact model version did this eval run validate?” and blocks automated linking in release notes. What this PR does: 1) Adds required fields to the eval run manifest/results payload: model_version (string) and model_metadata_schema_hash (sha256 hex). 2) Populates these fields from the run config when available, and falls back to resolving via the model registry client (best-effort) wh...

title: Add Private observability smoke-test harness (kind + Helm) and gate on core signals
preview: Motivation: We keep finding regressions in Private installs where Prometheus scrapes succeed but dashboards/rules don’t render (missing ConfigMaps), or metrics rename breaks the minimum pack. This PR adds a deterministic smoke-test harness that deploys a minimal Private stack into a fresh kind cluster and asserts th...
text:
Motivation: We keep finding regressions in Private installs where Prometheus scrapes succeed but dashboards/rules don’t render (missing ConfigMaps), or metrics rename breaks the minimum pack. This PR adds a deterministic smoke-test harness that deploys a minimal Private stack into a fresh kind cluster and asserts the observability minimum pack is actually usable (scrapes + basic PromQL + dashboards + alert rules). This is intended to be the v1 gate for ENG-19156 and to prevent repeats of INT-11804 failures and SUP-49210-style issues.

What this adds: (1) `hack/private_observability_smoke/` harness that creates a kind cluster, installs dependencies (kube-prometheus-stack + grafana), installs Redwood Private via Helm (control-plane + gateway + one runtime deployment), then validates signals; (2) a Go-based validator binary that queries Prometheus HTTP API and checks for presence of expe...

title: Standardize metric labels and reduce cardinality for Private installs
preview: Context: Private installs run customer-managed Prometheus with tighter TSDB/storage limits and stricter cardinality constraints than our hosted environments. We observed high series churn from per-request and per-route/model labels, especially when customers have many routes, frequently changing model versions, or m...
text: Context: Private installs run customer-managed Prometheus with tighter TSDB/storage limits and stricter cardinality constraints than our hosted environments. We observed high series churn from per-request and per-route/model labels, especially when customers have many routes, frequently changing model versions, or multi-tenant traffic. This PR standardizes label names across control plane + data plane services and introduces bounded label strategies for Private (while keeping a compatibility path for existing dashboards/alerts). Scope: (1) Label normalization: unify common label keys across services (service/component/namespace/cluster), rename inconsistent keys (route vs endpoint, model vs model_name), and remove labels that should never exist on metrics (request_id, raw path). (2) Cardinality reduction for Private: replace unbounded labels with bounded dimensions: route_group (confi...

title: Dedicated autoscaler: add metrics + structured logs for desired vs bounded capacity and prediction error
preview: Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired rep...
text:
Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired replicas vs bounded desired replicas, (2) bound-hit counters (clamped to min/max), (3) prediction error and staleness when predictive mode is enabled, and (4) an explicit scale-event reason enum that shows up in both metrics and logs.

What’s in this PR:
- New gauge metrics (emitted every autoscaler tick per deployment/region):
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.desired_replicas (the raw desired output from the decision function)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bounded_desired_replicas (after applying min/max + safety floors)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.current_replicas
 - re...

title: Export benchmark results as versioned CSV/JSON artifacts for web ingestion (schema + changelog)
preview: Context: Marketing site + interactive benchmark table needs a stable, versioned export of benchmark results that can be ingested by the web build pipeline and served via the results service (ENG-18566). This PR adds an export step that materializes benchmark outputs into (1) a canonical JSON bundle and (2) a CSV for...
text:
Context: Marketing site + interactive benchmark table needs a stable, versioned export of benchmark results that can be ingested by the web build pipeline and served via the results service (ENG-18566). This PR adds an export step that materializes benchmark outputs into (1) a canonical JSON bundle and (2) a CSV for quick inspection / spreadsheet workflows. Both artifacts are versioned, have an explicit schema version, and include a machine-readable changelog so the docs/web layer can safely detect and adapt to changes.

What’s included: - New exporter module (export/public_artifacts) that takes validated run aggregates and emits: - artifacts/public/v1/results.json (canonical) - artifacts/public/v1/results.csv (flat, human-friendly) - artifacts/public/v1/changelog.json (schema + field-level changes) - artifacts/public/v1/manifest.json (build metadata: git sha, created_at, prompt_set, ...

title: Rollout canary safety smoke tests (cohorting + auto-stop wiring) for staging
preview: Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO breach evaluates but orchestrator doesn’t pause/rollback, or rollout step stalls on metric timeouts). These tests are intentionally narrow, deterministic, and cleanup-safe so they can be run as part of GA change management.

Motivation / context: During beta we saw an incident-like split mismatch in us-east (SUP-48377) and a separate class of failures where metric evaluation timeouts could stall progress (see follow-ups in ENG-9158 / pr-28895). We already have E2E coverage in the monorepo (ENG-9164), but we wanted a lightweight “canary of the canary” that runs against stag...

title: Add e2e integration tests with Splunk HEC + Datadog Logs sandboxes
preview: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integr...
text: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need repeatable end-to-end confidence that the exporter can deliver to downstreams with realistic failure modes (auth errors, 429 throttling, payload validation issues) and that our retry/backoff + checkpointing semantics behave as expected. This PR adds an integration test suite + docker-compose-ish harness that boots the exporter alongside (a) a Splunk-like HEC stub service and (b) a Datadog Logs intake stub service. It does not attempt to fully emulate Splunk/Datadog; it’s focused on request/response behavior the adapters depend on. Scope: - New `e2e/` harness with compose config for local + CI runs - Splunk HEC stub: validates token header, supports `/services/collector/event` + `/services/collector/health`, can simulate 429s and partial failures - Datadog stub: validates API key header, supports `/api/v2/logs`, can simulate 403...

title: Add Audit Exporter Grafana dashboards + alert rules (lag, delivery errors)
preview: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to...
text: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to hosted + dedicated environments for the beta cohort. Links: Linear ENG-5168 (exporter observability), Confluence SLO doc: "Audit exporter SLOs and alerting" (owner: Sean/Rafael). Scope: (1) Grafana dashboard: Overview (lag, throughput, success rate), (2) Grafana dashboard: Destination adapters (Splunk HEC / Datadog / S3), (3) Alert rules: export lag burn, sustained adapter errors, DLQ growth, auto-paused exports, (4) Recording rules for rate-based panels to reduce query cost. Notes on semantics: Export lag is computed from exporter checkpoint watermark vs event time; alert...

title: Add canary for telemetry query latency (incident-bot diagnostics guardrail)
preview:
## Context
Incident-bot v2 relies on the telemetry query service to render saturation/context cards in the first ~60–90s of an incident thread. We’ve seen query latency spikes during incidents (bursty usage + upstream timeouts) that cause empty/partial cards and slow responder workflows. This PR adds a dedicated per...
text:
## Context
Incident-bot v2 relies on the telemetry query service to render saturation/context cards in the first ~60–90s of an incident thread. We’ve seen query latency spikes during incidents (bursty usage + upstream timeouts) that cause empty/partial cards and slow responder workflows. This PR adds a dedicated perf canary to continuously validate telemetry query latency and error behavior under a representative diagnostic workload.

Related:
- INT-3341 (telemetry query timeouts from incident-bot)
- ENG-5427 (telemetry query cache/coalescing)
- ENG-5429 (bot degraded-mode + load shedding)

## What this adds
1) New canary: `telemetry-query-latency`
- Runs every 60s (configurable) from 3 regions: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1
- Executes a fixed set of low-cardinality queries that mirror incident-bot cards:
 - GPU utilization (pool-level, region scoped)
 - Queue depth / scheduler back...

title: Add runbook links to experiment alerts + Console guardrail banners
preview: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident s...
text: Problem: During beta we saw multiple slow triage loops where the on-call (and Support) had to ask around for the right runbook when an experiment guardrail alert fired (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant skew, etc.). We already have runbooks in Confluence, but the link isn’t present where the incident starts: alert payloads and the Console guardrail banners. This PR wires canonical runbook URLs into (1) alert payload annotations (so PagerDuty/Slack notifications carry a direct ‘Runbook’ link) and (2) Console guardrail banners on the experiment details/results pages. Scope: route experiments only (no global SRE alert conventions refactor in this PR). What changed: - Added a small, versioned mapping of {alert_key -> runbook_url} for experiment-related alerts (propagation lag, attribution drop rate, variant 0 traffic, allocation skew, p99 regression auto-stop). - Plumbed run...

title: Add exporter metrics (lag/retries/throughput), basic tracing, and structured logs
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, ad...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-pass Prometheus metrics + basic tracing + structured logging to the audit-log-exporter service. Metrics are aligned to Redwood observability naming/cardinality standards (per Allison/Logan feedback) and provide the signals needed for initial dashboards/alerts (lag, throughput, retries, adapter errors). This work is scoped to exporter-side instrumentation only; the control plane status endpoint will consume these signals via existing health paths and the observability pack dashboards will be done separately. Context: ENG-5168. Motivation: During beta soak we’ve been debugging exporter lag spikes and downstream 429/backpressure mostly via ad-hoc logs. We need stable metrics to (a) quantify export lag vs SLO, (b) separate downstream throttling from internal queueing, (c) support paging policy (page on sustained lag / hard failures, ticket on transient retries)...

title: Add production readiness quickstart aligned with Console onboarding checklist
preview: This adds a new end-to-end “Production Readiness” quickstart intended to mirror the Console onboarding / first production launch checklist. The quickstart walks a new Hosted API user from: API key setup → first request → streaming → retries/timeouts → usage visibility → quotas/budgets guardrails → basic observabilit...
text: This adds a new end-to-end “Production Readiness” quickstart intended to mirror the Console onboarding / first production launch checklist. The quickstart walks a new Hosted API user from: API key setup → first request → streaming → retries/timeouts → usage visibility → quotas/budgets guardrails → basic observability hooks → model pinning + fallback (conceptual; uses config placeholders). Motivation: we keep seeing early prod launches fail on 401/403 setup mistakes, streaming disconnect confusion, and 429 surprises; the checklist in Console helps, but we need a canonical runnable path customers can copy/paste and deploy. Scope: adds a new quickstart directory with Python + TypeScript examples (Go is stubbed with TODO and links to the SDK guide), adds a minimal “healthcheck” endpoint pattern, structured logs, and a tiny dashboard note for usage-lag expectations. Includes guidance for w...

title: Add onboarding KPIs dashboard (TTFS/TTFP + checklist funnel) and recommended alerts
preview:
## Summary
Adds a packaged Grafana dashboard + alert rules for the Guided Onboarding / First Production Launch initiative.

This is intended to be imported via the standard `observability-pack` rollout (same mechanism as the latency/cost packs), and targets the onboarding telemetry datasets produced by the gateway/c...
text:
## Summary
Adds a packaged Grafana dashboard + alert rules for the Guided Onboarding / First Production Launch initiative.

This is intended to be imported via the standard `observability-pack` rollout (same mechanism as the latency/cost packs), and targets the onboarding telemetry datasets produced by the gateway/control plane eventing work + derived milestone tables.

## What’s included
Dashboard: **Onboarding KPIs (Hosted API)**
- TTFS (signup → first successful 2xx request) trend
 - Median / p90 / p95 by signup cohort
 - Breakdown by `sdk_language` (via user-agent tags), `region`, and `plan_tier`
- TTFP (signup → first production traffic) trend
 - Median / p90 by signup cohort
 - Includes optional filter for "prod_traffic" classifier (see notes below)
- Step completion funnel
 - % of tenants completing each checklist step (ordered)
 - Step-to-next-step conversion + time-in-step
 -...

title: Fix timeout handling in telemetry queries (retries/backoff + better degraded-mode messaging)
preview: Context: During incidents we often burst telemetry queries (GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure). When telemetry is slow or partially degraded, the bot currently either (a) times out and shows empty cards, or (b) holds the Slack interaction long enough that users think the bot is broken....
text:
Context: During incidents we often burst telemetry queries (GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure). When telemetry is slow or partially degraded, the bot currently either (a) times out and shows empty cards, or (b) holds the Slack interaction long enough that users think the bot is broken. This came up in INT-3341 and in recent #eng-infra / #eng-sre threads about bounding diagnostics load.

What this PR changes: 1) Adds a hard end-to-end deadline for telemetry collection per card render (default 4.5s, configurable), with per-query timeouts and cancellation propagation so we don’t keep hammering telemetry after the user path has moved on. 2) Implements bounded retries with exponential backoff + jitter for retryable classes (timeouts, 429, 502/503/504). Retries are capped (max 2) and respect the overall deadline. 3) Improves degraded-mode messaging in Slack ca...

title: Publish baseline vs quant-profile cost/token + latency deltas (CSV summaries + artifacts)
preview: Context: As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need a repeatable, reviewable set of benchmark outputs that quantify the impact of enabling a published quantization profile vs baseline precision on (a) unit economics (approx cost/token) and (b) latency/throughput across supported GPU SKUs. This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need a repeatable, reviewable set of benchmark outputs that quantify the impact of enabling a published quantization profile vs baseline precision on (a) unit economics (approx cost/token) and (b) latency/throughput across supported GPU SKUs. This PR adds a small publishing pipeline in benchmark-lab that generates normalized CSV summaries per model/profile/GPU and uploads both raw run logs and the rolled-up tables as CI artifacts. The goal is to let Applied ML + Runtime + Platform point to a single source of truth for “expected deltas” used by Console previews, rollout gates, and incident triage.

What’s included: (1) A new runner entrypoint that executes baseline + profile-enabled scenarios back-to-back with consistent warmup and pinned runtime build/commit; (2) CSV summaries under artifacts/quant_profiles/ summarizing ...

title: Include authz decision context in SIEM export (ECS/CIM mapping)
preview:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot cust...
text:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot customer requirements where SIEM rules need to distinguish auth failures (deny) from application errors, and correlate access decisions with specific permission strings.

Context
- Redwood platform services are adding authz decision fields to audit log events (see redwood monorepo PR: redwood#28292).
- RBAC v2 requires evidence-friendly audit trails (SOC2 + enterprise questionnaires).
- Customers commonly ingest our audit logs into Splunk (CIM) and/or Elastic (ECS). They want stable field names, consistent typing, and predictable nullability.

What changed
1) New exported field...

title: Tag eval outputs with experiment variant identifiers + publish compact score stream for Console
preview: Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1)...
text:
Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1) propagates experiment attribution fields into eval execution and output artifacts, and (2) publishes a compact score stream suitable for near-real-time aggregation and dashboarding.

What’s included: (A) Variant tagging: add optional experiment context to eval runs, including experiment_id, variant_id, route_id, and a stable stickiness_key_hash (when provided), and persist these tags into (i) run metadata.json, (ii) per-prompt result rows, and (iii) summary.json. (B) Compact score stream: emit one record per {run_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name} with normalized score, raw s...

title: Add rollout cohort panels (latency + errors) and alert templates
preview: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” d...
text: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” dashboard to the observability pack, plus alert templates and recommended default thresholds for auto-stop/rollback wiring. What’s included: (1) New dashboard: “Hosted Rollouts / Cohort Comparison (Latency + Errors)” with panels for request rate, error rate, timeout rate, p50/p95 latency, and a baseline-vs-canary delta view. Panels are parameterized around {route, region, rollout_id} and assume cohort in {baseline, canary}. (2) Library panels (Jsonnet) that can be embedded into other service dashboards: p95 latency by cohort, 5xx rate by cohort, timeouts by cohort, and “cana...

title: Security: destination validation + egress allowlist hooks (SSRF/exfil mitigation)
preview: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some dest...
text: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some destination fields in the control plane, but we want the exporter to be safe even if a bad config slips through (migration bugs, stale cached config, or future destinations). Summary of changes: 1) Strict URL parsing + normalization: Introduce a shared DestinationURL parser that rejects ambiguous/invalid URLs, strips userinfo, normalizes scheme/host/port, and prevents path tricks (e.g., embedded @, backslashes, encoded host separators). 2) Blocklist for dangerous targets: Reject localhost, link-local, and RFC1918 / private ranges (including 127.0.0.0/8, ::1, 10.0.0.0/8, 172.16....

title: Add Grafana dashboard: Route Experiment Guardrails (latency/error/cost + attribution drop)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, ...
text:
## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, or cost? (2) Are we missing experiment attribution (drop rate) such that results are untrustworthy? (3) Is traffic split behaving as expected (e.g., variant getting ~0% traffic)?

This dashboard complements the control-plane/Console views by providing a fast, infrastructure-centric view over raw telemetry, and is designed to work with the canonical attribution dimensions from ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region, model_id) while limiting cardinality blast-radius.

## What’s included ### Dashboard: "Route Experiments / Guardrails" Panels (high-level): - Overv...

title: Add ingestion-side cardinality guards for cache telemetry
preview:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache sign...
text:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache signals by the dimensions we actually operate on (model, route, deployment mode, region, and prompt-length/token bins).

This is the ingestion counterpart to runtime tagging + allowlists (see redwood/pr-18426) and ensures that even if a client or misconfigured gateway emits unexpected label keys/values, we either (a) rewrite to a safe aggregated bucket, or (b) drop with explicit counters so we can diagnose.

## Context / Motivation
During early canary of cache telemetry dashboards we saw a few failure modes:
- `tenant_id` and raw `route` occasionally showing up due to legacy ga...

title: Add cache telemetry overhead perf-canary gate (p95 regression threshold)
preview: Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exce...
text:
Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exceeds the configured threshold, the canary fails to prevent rollouts that silently add latency overhead.

Motivation: We’re rolling out new cache metrics (hit/miss counters, eviction pressure, wasted prefill) behind flags and with sampling, but we need a hard stop in CI to keep instrumentation overhead near-zero (especially at p95/p99). This PR is intended to be the guardrail referenced by the runtime rollout plan and SRE approval thread.

What’s included: (1) New scenario `cache_telemetry_overhead` that replays a prompt set designed to exercise prefill, decode, prefix reuse,...

title: Reconcile prefix cache vs KV cache metric naming (standards + ADR alignment)
preview:
## Summary
This PR reconciles naming between "prefix cache" vs "KV cache" metrics in the serving runtime and standardizes metric + label names to match:
- Observability standards: `cache-metrics-naming-and-cardinality-policy`
- ADR: `adr-prefix-kv-cache-hit-rate-definition`

Net effect: dashboards/alerts can rely on...
text:
## Summary
This PR reconciles naming between "prefix cache" vs "KV cache" metrics in the serving runtime and standardizes metric + label names to match:
- Observability standards: `cache-metrics-naming-and-cardinality-policy`
- ADR: `adr-prefix-kv-cache-hit-rate-definition`

Net effect: dashboards/alerts can rely on one stable namespace and avoid ambiguous (and occasionally inverted) terminology that crept in across earlier instrumentation PRs.

## Motivation / context
During ENG-4821 review + early dashboard wireframes, we found:
- Some metrics used `prefix_cache_*` for what was actually KV reuse (post-prefill), while others used `kv_cache_*` for prefix reuse during prefill.
- Counters were inconsistently suffixed (`_count` vs `_total`) and gauges used mixed units.
- Label keys drifted (`model` vs `model_id`, `route` vs `route_id`, `deployment` vs `deployment_mode`).

This made it ha...

title: Docs: add cache telemetry metric interpretation + dashboard links to internal README
preview: This is a small documentation-only PR to make the new prefix/KV cache telemetry usable by oncall and platform operators without having to hunt through Slack threads or dashboard PRs. It adds an internal README section that (1) defines the cache metrics in plain language (including what counts as hit/miss, partial re...
text:
This is a small documentation-only PR to make the new prefix/KV cache telemetry usable by oncall and platform operators without having to hunt through Slack threads or dashboard PRs. It adds an internal README section that (1) defines the cache metrics in plain language (including what counts as hit/miss, partial reuse, and how streaming retries are handled), (2) provides “how to interpret” guidance with common failure modes (capacity/eviction pressure, TTL expiry, policy disabled, hash/version mismatch), (3) documents label/aggregation expectations to avoid cardinality surprises, and (4) links directly to the dashboards + runbook.

Motivation: We’ve had a few iterations on naming + semantics (see the ADR and the recent rename PR) and SRE asked for a single canonical place to point oncall when hit-rate dips or when metrics appear to go missing.

What’s included: - New README section: ...

title: Add standardized incident-context dashboards (deploy timeline, saturation overview, error budget burn)
preview: This PR adds a standardized set of dashboards intended to be linked directly from Incident Bot v2 context cards. The goal is to make the bot output actionable in the first 5 minutes by providing consistent deep links (per service/region/route/time window) for deploy correlation, saturation triage, and SLO/error-budg...
text: This PR adds a standardized set of dashboards intended to be linked directly from Incident Bot v2 context cards. The goal is to make the bot output actionable in the first 5 minutes by providing consistent deep links (per service/region/route/time window) for deploy correlation, saturation triage, and SLO/error-budget burn. The dashboards are designed to be safe/default-bounded (limited time range defaults, low-cardinality variables, and guardrails around tenant labels) and to work across Hosted + Dedicated pools where the standard metrics exist. Included dashboards: (1) Deploy Timeline (service/region) — deploy events overlay (canary vs full rollout), build SHA, and error/latency overlays; (2) Saturation Overview (pool/region) — GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure/admission, and request concurrency; (3) Error Budget Burn (service/route/region) — 1h/6h bur...

title: Propagate route + model version context into runtime cache metrics (with cardinality allowlists)
preview:
## Context
We’re landing the cache telemetry suite (hit/miss, reuse depth, evictions, wasted prefill). For the signals to be actionable in dashboards and in the recommendations MVP, we need consistent slicing keys that match how Redwood operates: route, model, model version, deployment mode, and region.

This PR wir...
text:
## Context
We’re landing the cache telemetry suite (hit/miss, reuse depth, evictions, wasted prefill). For the signals to be actionable in dashboards and in the recommendations MVP, we need consistent slicing keys that match how Redwood operates: route, model, model version, deployment mode, and region.

This PR wires request-scoped context from the gateway/control plane into the runtime metrics emitter and applies strict allowlists/coercion so we don’t accidentally create per-tenant/per-request label explosions.

Related: ENG-4823 (route/model context joinability), ENG-4821 (metric defs + tags).

## What this changes
- Adds `TelemetryContext` (derived from `RequestContext`) to runtime metric emission sites for cache metrics.
- Adds the following metric labels (when enabled):
 - `rw_route`: stable route identifier (normalized, allowlisted; NOT tenant-specific)
 - `rw_model`: model ide...

title: Alerts: experiment config propagation lag + attribution anomalies (route experiments)
preview: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew ...
text: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew early in rollout). This PR adds Prometheus/Alertmanager rules and annotations for the standard route experiments dashboards, with SRE-reviewed runbook links and recommended severities. Scope: (1) propagation lag/staleness alerts tied to the new propagation lag metric emitted by the redwood monorepo, (2) attribution drop-rate and missing-dimension alerts for experiment metrics/traces aggregation, (3) “variant 0 traffic” detection to catch misconfigured allocations or eligibility rules. Notes: rules are written to avoid cardinality blow-ups (no per-route fanout by default); h...

title: Cache telemetry: add sampling controls + local rate limiting for high-volume events
preview: Problem: the new cache telemetry (hit/miss counters, eviction events, wasted prefill) is intentionally emitted at request boundaries and (in a few places) per-cache-event to support attribution. Under some workloads (streaming chat retries, high QPS short prompts, and continuous batching), this can become a high-vol...
text:
Problem: the new cache telemetry (hit/miss counters, eviction events, wasted prefill) is intentionally emitted at request boundaries and (in a few places) per-cache-event to support attribution. Under some workloads (streaming chat retries, high QPS short prompts, and continuous batching), this can become a high-volume signal source. We need guardrails so we don’t introduce p95/p99 latency overhead in runtime or create a sudden telemetry cost spike in prod.

This PR adds two layers of protection:
1) Deterministic sampling for event-style cache signals (and optionally for a subset of histograms) with stable sampling keys so ratios remain interpretable at aggregation time.
2) A lightweight local rate limiter in the runtime emitter path that caps the maximum number of cache telemetry emissions per process per interval, with self-observability metrics for drops.

What’s included (high lev...

title: Add dashboard panels + metric exports for quantization profile rollouts
preview:
Context
We’re rolling out route-level quantization profiles behind opt-in controls. SRE + design partners asked for a first-class dashboard view that answers: (1) what % of traffic is profile-enabled, (2) how often we fall back due to incompatibility/kill-switch, and (3) whether tail latency regresses when profiles ...
text:
Context
We’re rolling out route-level quantization profiles behind opt-in controls. SRE + design partners asked for a first-class dashboard view that answers: (1) what % of traffic is profile-enabled, (2) how often we fall back due to incompatibility/kill-switch, and (3) whether tail latency regresses when profiles are enabled.

This PR updates the observability pack references and metric exports used by the rollout dashboards. The focus is making the dashboards work out-of-the-box across hosted + dedicated, and ensuring slices are stable and cheap (avoid high-cardinality tags).

What changed
- Adds a new “Quantization Profile Rollout” dashboard group with panels:
 - Enabled rate (requests with quantization_profile_resolution=applied / total)
 - Fallback rate (quantization_profile_resolution=fallback / total) with breakdown by fallback_reason
 - p95 end-to-end latency (baseline vs ena...

title: Fix missing experiment attribution on streaming retries
preview: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was e...
text: Context: During the route experiments beta we saw a subset of streaming requests (SSE) lose experiment attribution after a retry, which caused (a) undercounted per-variant traffic, (b) misleading guardrail charts, and (c) confusing traces where the initial attempt was tagged but the retry attempt was not. This was escalated via SUP-24877 and corroborated by staging attribution-drop dashboards. Root cause: Our streaming retry path reconstructs a new RequestContext / SpanContext for the retry attempt. The base routing decision (including experiment assignment) was persisted on the first attempt, but the retry code path re-evaluated portions of the request pipeline and did not copy experiment dimensions into the retry context. In some cases we also emitted the streaming response headers from the retry attempt before experiment tags were re-attached, so x-redwood-experiment-id / x-redwood...
links: Jira SUP-24877: Missing experiment attribution on streaming
Slack thread: #support escalation referencing missing experiment_id on SSE retries
Grafana: Route Experiments / Attribution Drop Rate dashboard (staging)

title: Slack UX: modal pagination for large context + per-thread caching for repeated interactions
preview: Problem: large config diffs and saturation signal payloads frequently exceed what fits cleanly in a single Slack message block set. In practice responders were seeing (a) aggressive truncation that hid the one line they needed, and (b) repeated clicks/refreshes in a thread that re-ran expensive upstream queries, whi...
text:
Problem: large config diffs and saturation signal payloads frequently exceed what fits cleanly in a single Slack message block set. In practice responders were seeing (a) aggressive truncation that hid the one line they needed, and (b) repeated clicks/refreshes in a thread that re-ran expensive upstream queries, which is the opposite of what we want during an incident.

This PR introduces two changes for incident-bot v2 diagnostics rendering:

1) Modal pagination for large content (diffs + signal tables)
- Adds a "View details" button on cards that opens a Slack modal.
- Modal supports pagination (prev/next) with page size tuned for Slack's block/text limits.
- Supports both "unified" diff rendering and "key/value change list" rendering (whichever the card produced), using the same pagination component.
- Retains the existing short preview in the thread for quick scanning.

2) Per-thr...

title: Add recommended alert rules + SLO examples for Quickstart Template apps
preview: Context: As part of the Quickstart Templates launch, we want to ship a recommended alerting baseline that users can enable without getting paged to death. This PR adds an opt-in alert rule group for template apps (RAG/agents) plus example SLOs and burn-rate alerts. The rules are designed around the metrics emitted b...
text: Context: As part of the Quickstart Templates launch, we want to ship a recommended alerting baseline that users can enable without getting paged to death. This PR adds an opt-in alert rule group for template apps (RAG/agents) plus example SLOs and burn-rate alerts. The rules are designed around the metrics emitted by the templates + OTel collector configs from PR-156/158, and they intentionally avoid high-cardinality dimensions (no user_id, prompt hash, raw path, or tool args). What’s included: (1) SLO examples: availability + latency SLO recording rules with suggested targets and notes on how to adapt per workload; (2) Alert rules: multi-window burn-rate (fast/slow) alerts for the SLOs, plus a small set of “hard failures” (collector down, exporter errors) and “symptom alerts” (tool failure spikes, streaming disconnect spikes, retrieval empty-result spikes) with conservative defaults;...

title: Add log/trace correlation deep links for incident context
preview: Summary: Adds standardized log/trace correlation shortcuts (deep links) that incident-bot v2 and Console can embed for fast drill-down during incidents. This PR introduces a small, versioned link-template bundle in observability-pack plus validation tests to ensure URLs remain stable across releases. Motivation: In ...
text: Summary: Adds standardized log/trace correlation shortcuts (deep links) that incident-bot v2 and Console can embed for fast drill-down during incidents. This PR introduces a small, versioned link-template bundle in observability-pack plus validation tests to ensure URLs remain stable across releases. Motivation: In beta incident-bot v2 threads we repeatedly saw responders copy/paste request IDs and hunt manually across systems. We already link to dashboards (burn rate, deploy timeline, saturation), but we were missing the 1-click “take me to logs for this trace/request in the right time window” experience. This closes that gap while keeping URLs tenant-safe. Details: (1) Adds link templates for Traces and Logs with consistent parameter names and time windows, with support for service/route/region, optional trace_id, and optional request_id. (2) Adds a tiny resolver helper (used by bot...

title: Template dashboards v1: RAG + Agents + recommended alert rules (naming + publish steps)
preview: Context: DevEx Quickstart Templates (RAG + agents) are standardizing OTel instrumentation and a small set of Redwood-specific metrics/attributes. This PR ships the initial dashboard + alerts pack in `observability-pack` so template apps have a first-run observability experience and internal Support/SRE can quickly t...
text: Context: DevEx Quickstart Templates (RAG + agents) are standardizing OTel instrumentation and a small set of Redwood-specific metrics/attributes. This PR ships the initial dashboard + alerts pack in `observability-pack` so template apps have a first-run observability experience and internal Support/SRE can quickly triage common failures (401/429/timeouts/tool errors/retrieval empty). Scope: (1) dashboards (Grafana JSON) and (2) alert rules (Prometheus-style) + (3) documentation for metric naming conventions, required labels, and publish steps for release tagging. Dashboards included: (a) `Templates / RAG + Agents Overview` (golden signals + error taxonomy rollup) (b) `Templates / RAG Latency Breakdown` (gateway + model + retrieval + tool time; p50/p95; streaming disconnect rate) (c) `Templates / Retrieval Health` (retrieval hit rate, empty result rate, top-k distribution, chunking def...

title: Add TP stall dashboard panels + alert rules (heartbeat gaps, stall reasons, timeout correlation)
preview:
Context
We’ve had repeated Dedicated + high-throughput Hosted incidents where TP replicas appear healthy at the process level but are effectively wedged: requests hang, streaming timeouts rise, and autoscaling thrashes. Runtime-side instrumentation is landing in parallel (ENG-4823 / runtime PRs), but we need a first...
text:
Context
We’ve had repeated Dedicated + high-throughput Hosted incidents where TP replicas appear healthy at the process level but are effectively wedged: requests hang, streaming timeouts rise, and autoscaling thrashes. Runtime-side instrumentation is landing in parallel (ENG-4823 / runtime PRs), but we need a first-class dashboard + alerting layer to detect silent stalls early and make triage actionable.

This PR adds:
- Grafana dashboard panels for TP heartbeat gaps (per rank + aggregated), stall reason counts, and request timeout correlation by model/cluster/replica.
- Prometheus alert rules with sane defaults + paging policy alignment (page only on sustained stall/timeout correlation, ticket otherwise).
- Links from alerts to the on-call runbook and to the most relevant filters (cluster/account/model).

What’s included (panels)
1) TP Heartbeat
- Panel: “TP heartbeat age (p50/p95/p...

title: Console: add usage analytics filter for template-tagged traffic
preview:
Title: Console: add usage analytics filter for template-tagged traffic

Context
DevEx quickstart templates optionally tag API traffic so we can measure adoption/activation without relying on inference logs or user self-reporting (PM-912). PR-18421 added a whitelist + validation for the template telemetry headers at ...
text:
Title: Console: add usage analytics filter for template-tagged traffic

Context
DevEx quickstart templates optionally tag API traffic so we can measure adoption/activation without relying on inference logs or user self-reporting (PM-912). PR-18421 added a whitelist + validation for the template telemetry headers at the gateway. This PR completes the loop on the Console side by adding a first-class filter in Usage Analytics dashboards to view only “template-generated” traffic.

Goals
- Add a low-cardinality, privacy-safe way to filter usage analytics to template-tagged traffic.
- Keep existing dashboards/backfills working for customers that do not send the template tag.
- Avoid introducing any tag values or user-provided strings into analytics dimensions.

Non-goals
- Building a new dashboard; this is a filter + query update on existing Usage Analytics views.
- Capturing template versi...

title: Add retention verification + compliance pack generation dashboards/alerts
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Gener...
text:
Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Generation SLIs (success rate + latency) with corresponding alerts. It also updates the repo README to document metric prerequisites and rollout guidance.

Context / why: We’ve had multiple incidents/tickets where enforcement was “working” but not observable at the right level (e.g., policy applied but TTL lag growing in a single region; purge jobs silently throttling after maintenance). Security/GRC also needs a customer-ready way to evidence enforcement (at least internally) and to demonstrate the compliance pack generator’s reliability as we start using it for enterprise secu...

title: Add Dedicated staged rollout dashboards + alert templates (stage p95/p99, error rate, GPU saturation, promotion annotations)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first cut of dashboard + alert templates for the Dedicated staged rollout system. The dashboards are stage-aware (stage_id / stage_name) and are designed to support the rollout gate model in the policy engine (latency, errors, saturation/headroom, GPU health proxies). It also adds annotations...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a first cut of dashboard + alert templates for the Dedicated staged rollout system. The dashboards are stage-aware (stage_id / stage_name) and are designed to support the rollout gate model in the policy engine (latency, errors, saturation/headroom, GPU health proxies). It also adds annotations for stage promotion/abort/hold events so operators can correlate metric shifts with rollout actions.

Context / dependencies: This is intended to ship alongside the redwood monorepo changes that (a) emit stage tags in metrics/traces and (b) provide audit events for promote/abort/hold. Dashboards assume the following labels are present on the relevant metrics: dedicated_pool_id, rollout_plan_id, stage_id, stage_name, region. For cardinality safety, the dashboards intentionally avoid any per-request labels and only group-by the allowlisted stage identifiers.

What’s included...

title: Telemetry: cardinality guardrails for unit economics tags
preview:
Summary
- Adds hard validation + enforcement guardrails for high-cardinality tag dimensions used by the Unit Economics pipeline (model_id/model_version, region, customer_tier, quantization_profile, route_group, streaming).
- Prevents accidental explosions in metrics cardinality (e.g. route_id, raw customer identifie...
text:
Summary
- Adds hard validation + enforcement guardrails for high-cardinality tag dimensions used by the Unit Economics pipeline (model_id/model_version, region, customer_tier, quantization_profile, route_group, streaming).
- Prevents accidental explosions in metrics cardinality (e.g. route_id, raw customer identifiers, arbitrary quantization profile strings) that have previously caused ingestion lag and warehouse view instability.
- Emits explicit counters for dropped/rewritten tags so we can alert before dashboard coverage degrades.

Context
The internal Unit Economics dashboard depends on stable metering dimensions (see Confluence: token-metering-dimensions-and-cardinality). Over the last few weeks we’ve seen several near-misses where a single deploy introduced unbounded values in a tag (route id with embedded customer UUID; quantization profile leaking experiment run ids), which th...

title: Runtime: load quantization profile artifacts, apply mode, and emit observability signals
preview: Summary: This PR wires the serving runtime to (1) consume the resolved quantization profile reference from routing metadata, (2) fetch and validate the corresponding profile metadata + artifacts from the model registry, (3) apply quantization mode deterministically for the request/model shard, and (4) emit metrics +...
text: Summary: This PR wires the serving runtime to (1) consume the resolved quantization profile reference from routing metadata, (2) fetch and validate the corresponding profile metadata + artifacts from the model registry, (3) apply quantization mode deterministically for the request/model shard, and (4) emit metrics + structured events so rollouts can be observed and debugged. Motivation: The Quantization Profile Library is opt-in and route-scoped. For v1 we need deterministic selection, safe fallback, and clear signals for SRE and customers to verify whether a profile was applied for a given request. This PR is scoped to runtime behavior only; control plane resolution/compatibility checks are handled upstream (see related PRs). Design overview: - Input contract: router attaches quantization selection metadata to the request context (grpc metadata + internal request proto field). Runtim...

title: Dedicated: tier-aware admission control guards (queue caps + overload shedding)
preview:
Context

Dedicated throughput guarantees require us to keep tail latency and per-deployment throughput stable even when a pool is oversubscribed (mis-sized customers, sudden bursts, noisy neighbors, or control-plane misroutes). Today, overload behavior is mostly “best effort” and can lead to queue buildup that drags...
text:
Context

Dedicated throughput guarantees require us to keep tail latency and per-deployment throughput stable even when a pool is oversubscribed (mis-sized customers, sudden bursts, noisy neighbors, or control-plane misroutes). Today, overload behavior is mostly “best effort” and can lead to queue buildup that drags the entire pool down.

This PR implements tier-aware admission control guards in the gateway->orchestrator path:
- queue caps per (deployment_id, route) with tier-specific ceilings
- overload shedding rules that preferentially shed lower-tier/burst traffic while protecting committed Dedicated throughput floors
- consistent, observable rejection semantics (429 vs 503) and reason codes

Links
- Linear: ENG-19257 (Tier-aware admission control)
- Related: ENG-19244 / PR-19321 (per-deployment throughput metrics), ENG-19263 / PR-19366 (tier->pool mapping)
- Support signal: SUP-1...

title: Gateway: whitelist + validate quickstart template telemetry headers
preview: Context: DevEx quickstart templates need a reliable, low-cardinality way to tag traffic as template-generated so we can measure activation funnel and time-to-first-success without relying on parsing user-agent strings. Today the API gateway drops unknown `x-*` headers by default; templates adding a telemetry header ...
text:
Context: DevEx quickstart templates need a reliable, low-cardinality way to tag traffic as template-generated so we can measure activation funnel and time-to-first-success without relying on parsing user-agent strings. Today the API gateway drops unknown `x-*` headers by default; templates adding a telemetry header would silently lose that tag. This PR introduces an explicit allowlist in the gateway for a small set of template telemetry headers and performs strict validation to prevent abuse, high-cardinality explosions, and accidental PII leakage.

What this does: Adds a `template telemetry` header allowlist and validator at the gateway edge. Allowed headers are normalized into a structured internal context object that can be consumed by downstream logging/metrics/analytics (follow-up PRs). Invalid values are rejected with a 400 (clear error message) to avoid partial/ambiguous teleme...

title: Dedicated: emit burst-capacity metering events (committed vs burst) for billing exports
preview: Context: Dedicated tiers require billing/usage export to separate committed throughput consumption from burst capacity so Finance/Sales/CS can explain invoices and enforce tier policies (ENG-19302). This PR introduces a canonical metering event for burst usage, wires it into the request accounting path, and adds a d...
text: Context: Dedicated tiers require billing/usage export to separate committed throughput consumption from burst capacity so Finance/Sales/CS can explain invoices and enforce tier policies (ENG-19302). This PR introduces a canonical metering event for burst usage, wires it into the request accounting path, and adds a daily aggregation job that produces committed_vs_burst totals per deployment_id/model/region. Definitions: (1) committed_usage_tokens = tokens attributed while the deployment is within its committed throughput floor capacity slice, (2) burst_usage_tokens = tokens attributed while the deployment consumes burst pool capacity above its committed slice, (3) unmetered_tokens = tokens excluded from billing exports (e.g., internal health checks, warmup). Attribution is computed at request finalization using the tier-aware admission/routing decision already attached to the request c...

title: Add NCCL collective counters and rank ids to TP tracing spans
preview: Summary: Extend TP tracing instrumentation to include (a) rank identifiers and (b) NCCL collective counters on relevant spans so we can correlate “silent stalls” across ranks/replicas and disambiguate scheduler starvation vs comms waits. This is part of the ENG-4823 instrumentation work and complements the new heart...
text:
Summary: Extend TP tracing instrumentation to include (a) rank identifiers and (b) NCCL collective counters on relevant spans so we can correlate “silent stalls” across ranks/replicas and disambiguate scheduler starvation vs comms waits. This is part of the ENG-4823 instrumentation work and complements the new heartbeat metrics by providing per-request, per-iteration context in traces.

Motivation / Background: During recent Dedicated incidents (see SUP-48377 and the 2025-02-17 Sev1 thread), we had cases where p99 latency spiked and requests timed out without a clear signal of whether we were stuck waiting on a collective, stuck on a CUDA event/stream wait, or simply starving collectives behind prefill-heavy work. Metrics help at fleet level, but when debugging a single wedged replica we need to answer: “Which collective were we on?”, “Did all ranks reach the same point?”, and “Are we...

title: Add saturation signal queries (GPU/queue/KV cache) to incident context payload
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new “Saturation signals” card to the incident-bot v2 context payload and Slack rendering. The card pulls a bounded set of telemetry signals for the affected service/region over a short window (default: last 15m) and summarizes them using simple thresholding so responders can quickly decide ...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new “Saturation signals” card to the incident-bot v2 context payload and Slack rendering. The card pulls a bounded set of telemetry signals for the affected service/region over a short window (default: last 15m) and summarizes them using simple thresholding so responders can quickly decide whether they’re dealing with capacity/scheduler pressure vs. an application/deploy regression.

Signals included (initial set): (1) GPU utilization (avg/p95), (2) scheduler/ingress queue depth (p95 + max), (3) KV cache pressure (p95), plus optional backpressure/admission-control indicators when present.

This is designed to be safe under incident conditions: strict timeouts, query budget enforcement, and an explicit degraded-mode response (cached snapshot + dashboard links) when live queries fail.

## Motivation
Multiple recent incidents (e.g., EU-west capacity saturation +...

title: Unit economics: warehouse view performance optimizations (partitioning + query plan fixes)
preview: This PR improves drilldown query latency for the internal Unit Economics dashboard by tightening warehouse table layout (partitioning/clustering), removing a few high-cost joins and DISTINCTs in the hot path, and switching the most commonly-hit rollups to pre-aggregated/materialized views. The primary goal is to kee...
text: This PR improves drilldown query latency for the internal Unit Economics dashboard by tightening warehouse table layout (partitioning/clustering), removing a few high-cost joins and DISTINCTs in the hot path, and switching the most commonly-hit rollups to pre-aggregated/materialized views. The primary goal is to keep p95 query time for time-series + top-N breakdowns under ~2s for typical Finance/Product filter combinations (7d/30d, group_by=model|region|tier) while keeping hourly freshness and correctness intact. Context: during dogfood we saw a few breakdowns regress to 10–25s when grouping by model+region with additional filters (quantization_profile, streaming) due to full scans on hourly fact tables and an inefficient join to the rate card + region mapping tables. This is follow-on work to the initial aggregation job (PR #19342) and API endpoints (PR #19374). Key changes: (1) Re-p...

title: Add canary check for output-token metering parity (streaming + non-streaming)
preview: Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a g...
text:
Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a gateway/runtime change is widely rolled out.

What this adds:
- New canary suite `metering_output_token_parity` that runs against a small allowlisted canary tenant in prod.
- Exercises BOTH streaming and non-streaming generation paths (including tool-call shaped outputs) and validates:
 1) output_tokens reported by the API response metadata
 2) output_tokens recorded by metering events (usage pipeline)
 3) that the delta between (1) and (2) is within a configured tolerance after accounting for ingestion latency
- Adds guardrails for ingestion lag: the canary polls the meteri...

title: Add data quality checks + anomaly alerts for unit economics pipeline
preview: Context: We’ve seen multiple cases where the Unit Economics dashboard showed sudden improvements or drops that were actually pipeline issues (most commonly ingestion lag or missing dimensions), e.g. the “hours missing” incident thread and earlier streaming token undercount investigations. This PR adds first-pass dat...
text:
Context: We’ve seen multiple cases where the Unit Economics dashboard showed sudden improvements or drops that were actually pipeline issues (most commonly ingestion lag or missing dimensions), e.g. the “hours missing” incident thread and earlier streaming token undercount investigations. This PR adds first-pass data quality checks and anomaly alerts for the hourly unit economics aggregation outputs so we can detect regressions quickly and avoid Finance/Product interpreting bad data.

What this PR does: (1) Completeness checks per hour: verifies presence of expected rows for core dimensions (region, customer_tier, model) and flags coverage below threshold; (2) Missing dimension checks: alerts when unknown/NULL region or customer_tier exceeds a configured percentage; (3) Token-volume anomaly checks: compares output_tokens to a trailing baseline (7d median by {region,tier} and global) a...
